transcript.full_transcript:
Zain Ali: Hi, mate. How are you? Yeah, very well, mate.

Daniel Brown: How are you?

Zain Ali: Yeah, I'm good. I'm good. Thank you. Good, good, good, good.

Daniel Brown: So thanks for jumping on, mate. Obviously, we've got a lot going on at the moment on preparation for this webinar. And as I said in my voice note the other day, there's going to be some big changes that we're going to be making inside WeBuildTrades. Just to try and take advantage of everything that's happening with AI. We never want to be in a position where we're that big that we can't pivot quickly. We always want to be obviously making sure that we're changing with the time. So I think we have made the decision that we're going to be basically pausing WeBuildTrades. Like anyone who wants to join, they'll join a wait list while we make this massive transition. And obviously then after the when we come out the other side of that, WeBuildTrades will be much more simple. We'll have a cleaner offer. It's going to make the whole team a lot more effective because everyone who jumps on a sales call will already know the price. And I'm going to be investing a lot of time into training all of the team in sales. So instead of just having jazz there, we've got the entire team whose daily KPIs is to contact a certain amount of businesses. This is the direction that we need to be moving towards if AI is going to be doing a lot of the heavy lifting on the back end.

Zain Ali: Yeah, sure. So a few projects which I'm currently working on, and most of them are like delivered. Some of them are working on and making continuous changes. So most important one was Closeboard, which clients are already using. Closeboard launched a Scorpion type of live chat widget, which we have started testing, delivered it to Daryl Keystone from Keystone property claims. Live chat was already available in GoHighLevel, but that was missing some of the messages. So we are trying Closeboard's own live chat widget. Second is the Voice agent again, which we have definitely gone through different iterations. And with your feedback, we have improved it more and more. So we would need your input on that. Maybe watch the demo, maybe have a couple calls with it.

Daniel Brown: Yeah, I'll definitely do that again. So at the moment, you need for me to go through the demo?

Zain Ali: Yes. Have you, I did watch a demo video. So inside our Trello board, we have Voice AI. I have delivered the latest demo. You just need to maybe call to this ABC landing number and webuildtrades number and have a couple calls with it. Let me know your feedback. And I'm excited to have this launched ASAP.

Daniel Brown: What I'm going to need to do here is I'm going to need that landing page. I need this to be like the version. I need you to basically get access to this version and make this live on the URL. Also, I want to have your previous version, because I think that's a really good use case of AI where I can show — look, this is what you produced and then me using AI was able to take that and turn it into this with just a couple of prompts. So yours would be like version one and this would be version two.

Zain Ali: So do you want to keep version one as well, maybe on some other domain?

Daniel Brown: Yeah, it could be on the same domain just as like a subfolder that says version.

Zain Ali: Okay, and just make sure this one's fully functional.

Daniel Brown: So my demo, when I record it, will sit here. Another thing — you mentioned to me the power of Claude or Codex or whatever to create these little branded videos, like little promotional videos. And I did see a company that uses a tool called Remotion. I'm not sure if you're familiar with it.

Zain Ali: Yeah, Remotion is similar to Hyperframes, but it's more powerful. So I need to build that skill basically. I tried it a few times and my team also tried it, so it doesn't generate like a high class output in first iteration, but it does pretty much a pretty good job as a version one. Like an ideation stage, which would take maybe weeks, but then we can definitely tweak it, do more iterations frame by frame, and then it'll get there.

Daniel Brown: Well, two things. First of all, we've got Cameron and the video editing team who can work on them. So that's fine. It'll be good to give them like a foundation to build from. But like literally, mate, it's only going to be like a month, it could be a couple of weeks, it could be a month, could be three months before the AI or remotion is able to produce better work than what the video editors will. So yeah, we want to just get ahead of all of these things.

Zain Ali: Yeah, it is. It's already becoming — it's just how we prompt it, how we give it context and all that.

Daniel Brown: So, mate, that's the voice AI. And obviously every project that we're working on, we would want one of these kind of branded videos for it. So we spoke about Closeboard and you're testing that. Spoke about the voice AI. What's next on your list?

Zain Ali: So the next is the automation for SEO and social media team. So I had delivered the dominant blog writer automation. I sent you the demo. That demo is available on Trello as well. So in our Trello board, we have this SEO AI project. I have delivered maybe two comments. So this was shared on WhatsApp. I just moved it to Trello as well. So this is the demo I have delivered to the social media team on Rocket where they can use this automation and generate the full month content with just by filling out a form.

Daniel Brown: What's the quality like that's coming out of it?

Zain Ali: Yeah, it's — let me just go to my mail and show you. Similar to the website content, we get another email which is for social media. So this is for Ashford. And it generates posts, which are caption and tags and phone number call to action. And then it would fetch through the reviews from the client side, their reviews page. And it would also make reviews posts as well. So eight review posts. And then there are 12 caption posts.

Daniel Brown: Let's look. Let's go through the caption ones one by one. I'd like to give some feedback on them. Some feedback here, mate. First of all, and I've spoken about this to Cameron, but again, if this AI is being trained on Cameron's posts, then it looks like Cameron hasn't applied my feedback. So first of all, way too many emojis. We have to remove the emoji at all.

Zain Ali: So Cameron gave us the feedback that he doesn't need emojis. So we are improving the automation to not include emojis. Is that what you wanted?

Daniel Brown: Yeah. So we don't want every post looking identical. Whereas at the moment, every post pretty much looks identical and it just looks like it's all been massively templated. So we want to have it where like some posts are long, some posts are short. Some posts have emojis, some posts don't have emojis. Make it random. We don't want to overuse emojis. And the one thing that's really important here is we never want to start a sentence with an emoji unless we're using an emoji as a bullet point. That is just so obvious that it's AI generated. And then I think that will just look a lot better because the goal really is, mate, is to make it look like these posts have been created by a human.

Zain Ali: Got it.

Daniel Brown: Now, I wouldn't have been able to have this conversation what I'm about to have like a week ago or two weeks ago. But now I can because the image quality that the ChatGPT is creating is absolutely insane. How we move to version two is actually having images as part of the posts. We need the clients to organize themselves in a way where all of their images that they're happy for us to use are inside a folder. And then those authentic images can actually feed into AI as well and then we use variations. Because this is the next step now — if we can get the AI to take care of the entire social media side of things, including the image generation, can you imagine what that's going to look like?

Zain Ali: Yeah. Got it. So images two from OpenAI was launched recently. So we'll include this part of the module into our automation. So it not only generates content, but also generates the images. Brilliant, mate.

Daniel Brown: And then also add a note on this for the blog posts as well. Version one of AI with what we're using was all about text. We've got website text, blog text, emails, and social media. But the level up from that now, which is where AI's capabilities came in, is image. So on all of these projects that we've worked on previously, we need to add image into it now. And that's going to be just an insane use case.

Zain Ali: Got it. So we can include images in social media writer, in blog writer. The blog post and social media is definitely number one priority. So it will have blogs, thumbnails, and any images to use inside the blog as well.

Daniel Brown: Correct. Yeah. And then obviously if we promote that blog on social media, it can have that post as well. Because again, mate, what we're looking at here now as well is we want to use what we're doing for our Dominate clients for WeBuildTrades as well and for the Trade Business School. So a bit of a side project for you here will be basically setting up this content engine for both WeBuildTrades and the Trade Business School. I think what would probably be good here is if we did a weekly YouTube video for WeBuildTrades that we promote across WeBuildTrades and Trade Business School. So on a Sunday, we take this one video of content, we use AI to turn it into a blog post, and we promote that same thing on the WeBuildTrades social media and the Trade Business School social media.

Zain Ali: And what that content would actually be? Would it be like coaching stuff or providing best tips or maybe just choosing a topic which is pain points to the businesses and then speaking about it?

Daniel Brown: I'm thinking more of like a weekly update video where it's like — because that's going to be more relevant. And then in that weekly update video I can share stories, I can share testimonials, share what's happening in AI. So like this is where it will be really good for on a weekly basis for everyone in the teams to send me an update on what they're working on. And then I turn that into content. So welcome to the WeBuild Trades Weekly Digest. In this video, I'm going to be showing you — so it's like a consistent format every week.

Zain Ali: Yeah, and one suggestion into this is that we need to also crawl some of the pain points businesses are facing maybe this week or this month online. Those could be from Reddit or Facebook groups. But in order for it to crawl, it would need to launch the Chrome MCP dev tool, and then you would need to log in your Facebook account. But have it safely or maybe create a new account so it doesn't get blocked because we are doing crawling.

Daniel Brown: So let's now take what we're talking about here. This is a really interesting idea. If I just share my screen here now. So the WeBuildTrades Command HQ is something that I'm going to be personally using. And we've got the different C-level officers — the COO, the CMO, the CFO, and CRO. So what you've just said there would basically be the CRO.

Zain Ali: Yeah.

Daniel Brown: So we can basically adjust and tailor the WeBuildTrades one to whatever we need. For example, on this content pipeline one, we could have a thing in here where every week, a message is sent out to you, Naren, and say Dom and Jazz to give an update. So you're giving me an update on AI. Naren's giving me an update on the command HQ and the websites. Dom's giving me an update on operationally across the business. And Jazz is giving me an update on sales and what's happening across the board, like exciting things that are happening.

Zain Ali: And then we have the SEO AI project as well. The blog writer agent is updated where it would research on the client side and research the metadata and then it would write the blog article and then this is the QC agent which would quality check it. So if it's less than seven, then it would give the feedback on what things to improve. And then it would regenerate the blog again. Then once it's more than seven, then it would store the blog, format it, and save it to Google Docs and send email.

Daniel Brown: And we have Command HQ Customer Hub app live on Google Store and Apple Store. So at the moment the version we had before with our new toolbox is switched over to Command HQ. And some of the more enhanced features such as invoices and bookings, those APIs, they are testing. We are now building Employee Hub app. So once that is done, we will push that as well to Google Store and Apple Store.

Zain Ali: And other than that, finally, last project is Sales AI. So in Sales AI, we have multiple projects. One is the discovery call preparation project. So this one is basically to prepare a report which Jazz can review before every discovery call. But because discovery call is more about knowing the business and what their challenges are, Jazz recommended that this won't bring any value. So I think we would need to pause this one, maybe, and not work on this one. Whereas the second project is proposal generator — we'll move towards the proposal generator then and call analyzer, which would analyze the call and then give feedback on the things to improve as we discussed.

Daniel Brown: That call analyzer one, mate, is going to be key because essentially we want AI to be the sales manager. And this will be just incredible for us.

Zain Ali: Exactly. And with this call analyzer — can I ask a few questions on this? Do we need Call Analyzer to analyze calls on all the departments and also keep a directory or maybe a folder where anybody can log into the portal and basically click on the client and see the departments. And each department has a set of calls and click on the department and then click on the team member. I think that would be beautiful.

Daniel Brown: If we could have that, then yeah, because then it's organized. So every type of call, it's organized by type of call, whether it's a discovery call, whether it's an ads intro call, whether it's a launch call, whether it's a follow-up call from Jazz to a client, or even whether it's a team call. And then, yeah, if the AI can analyze all of that, like just think about the amount of data that we get, but also it acts as a very powerful training tool. If we're going to be getting Ben and Ruben and Cole, who've never had no sales experience, hoping to do the sales calls, we want them to have active feedback that's coming in from all the calls that they're doing. So I listened to one of Cole's calls the other day, which was all around his first stage interview. And on the interviews, he was saying the word mate way too often. And like I've banned Jazz, Dom, and Cole from saying the word mate to anyone they're meeting for the first time because it's too friendly. Like you don't go to the doctors and the doctor sits you down in the chair and says, how are you doing, mate? Nice to meet you, mate. Like it completely takes away our authority and credibility and takes us out of the alpha position. So things like that was massive. The amount of times we say um or essentially was massive. Talking too much was massive where we should be on a sales call asking a question and shutting up and getting the answer and then trying to dig deeper into what their pain is. So then at the end of it, we can ask question, ask question, ask question, ask question, to then see if we can be the company that can prescribe the solution.

Zain Ali: Yeah, and on that one, some of the companies, bigger companies, they have some sort of tools which are giving them live suggestions during the meeting. Because they have so much data about a client, and they would just hit on the client — they would have a live feed, whatever client is speaking, and any questions to interrupt the client, they would just ask the question because they see everything on the screen.

Daniel Brown: Interesting. Mate, anything like this, 100%, anything that's going to help us improve is always going to be relevant for us to at least explore. So yeah, I'd be happy to try all that for sure.

Zain Ali: Okay, so I'll also see — the version one is to have the call analyzer, which analyzes the call after it has happened. And then maybe in version two, we can see what are the tools we can integrate for the live feed or maybe build our own tool which joins the meeting just like Fathom. And then it would suggest what the speaker needs to ask from the participant in real time. We'll see if Fathom has that. So they might have this already in their premium package, but we'll check on that as well.

Daniel Brown: Well, I saw that Fireflies, which is the alternative to Fathom, I did see that integrates with a lot of tools. So I'm not sure if Fathom does or whatever, but if we do need to change, that's fine. But again, just so you understand the goal here, Zain — we want to really just make sure that WeBuildTrades is the most AI-enhanced agency that exists in every department, sales, SEO, all of these different areas, because that's going to be the blueprint for agency disrupt. So yeah, very exciting, mate. Was there anything else that you wanted to go through on this one?

Zain Ali: No, I think I have covered all the projects. That's all.

Daniel Brown: Well, what I'll do, I'll send this over to Naren. We'll get an update from him. I am very excited on this content writer one that we spoke about. All of the other ones, mate, just keep doing what you're doing. Keep cracking on with them. I'll do the voice AI demo. I'll watch the SEO writer demo that you've mentioned as well. And then yeah, once Naren's watched this video, we'll get him on a call as well. And we will carry on that weekly meeting with him. What I need to do, mate, is actually create like a weekly meeting where we're all added. So it's just in the diary because I don't think I added that last time. So leave that with me. I'll pass this to Naren and then we'll get a weekly meeting on the go.

Daniel Brown: All right then, mate. Well, enjoy the rest of your day, Zain. Thanks for jumping on, mate, and I'll catch up with you soon.

Zain Ali: All right. Thank you so much. Have a good day. Bye. Thanks, mate. Bye-bye.

segments.speaker: Daniel Brown
segments.text: All right then, mate. Well, enjoy the rest of your day, Zain. Thanks for jumping on, mate, and I'll catch up with you soon.

segments.speaker: Zain Ali
segments.text: All right. Thank you so much. Have a good day. Bye. Thanks, mate. Bye-bye.
